神庙

rows.original: What can she tell you about the temple here?
rows.translation: 她能告诉你关于这座寺庙的哪些信息呢？
rows.raw: "What can she tell you about the temple here?"

rows.translation: 无梦

rows.original: What does she mean by Dreamless?
rows.translation: 她所说的“无梦”是什么意思呢？
这座城市

rows.original: Ynes isn't a native, that much you can tell just by her accent. So what's an outsider to think of the City of Glass?
rows.translation: 从伊内斯的口音就能明显听出来，她并不是这个地方的人。那么，对于一个外来者来说，玻璃之城会给他们留下怎样的印象呢？
rows.raw: "Ynes isn't a native, that much you can tell just by her accent. So what's an outsider to think of the City of Glass?"

rows.translation: 王室家族

rows.original: Does Ynes have any experience with the royal family?
rows.translation: 伊内斯是否有与王室打交道的经验呢？
rows.raw: "Does Ynes have any experience with the royal family?"

rows.translation: 瓦斯科大师

rows.original: What does Ynes know about Magister Vasko?
rows.translation: 伊内斯对瓦斯科大师了解多少呢？
rows.raw: "What does Ynes know about Magister Vasko?"

rows.translation: [ynes.numKids 2|孩子们|[ynes.kidName]]
[ynes.numKids 2|孩子们|[ynes.kidName]]
去看看你的……吧。 [ynes.numKids 1 2|儿子|孩子们].
返回

rows.original: You lean over the librarian's desk, peering underneath it. "Where's [ynes.kidName]?" Ynes rolls her eyes. "I hate to break it to you, but the clergy take a dim view of babies wailing in the sanctuary. [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] is |Our children are ] downstairs, with some of the other Dreamless. One of the most harmless of us all, Lenna, is a Khor'minoan — so milk is taken care of. And I manage to slip away to visit every hour or so. Don't worry. [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] is |Our children are ] well taken care of." "Can we go and visit right now?" Ynes gives you an indulgent look and pushes back from her desk. "Oh, very well, come along. I'm sure [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |the children ] will be happy to see you." You help Ynes up, and let her lead the way down to the servants' quarters underneath the temple. It's spartan down here, but warm despite the walls of stone thanks to woven tapestries and rugs reflecting the cultures of the Dreamless down here: you recognize the geometric patterns of Khor'minos, the bright colors of southern Belhar, icy hues of the cold north, and even fish-and-fire themes from the coastal realms. There are other children down here, too, running down down the halls and laughing — but these aren't the Dreamless's get. These are young Astrida, black-scaled salamanders with accents of azure and crimson, wearing little tartan togas adorned with silver oroboroses. [var0|You can't help but smile, knowing that someday your own children will be among them.|They're a bit too old to be playing with [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |your children ], though.] You pass them by, ruffling hair and swatting at errant tails, until you make your way to a black-painted door in the depths. "Here we are," Ynes says, heading in. "And there [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he is |they are ]." Inside is a simply-furnished sitting room built around a fireplace and chimney, in which a cowgirl's sitting with your son in her lap, nursing the hungry little lad with a smile on her face. "Oh, Ynes! And the Champion as well! Hello." You wave, kneeling down to get a peek at [ynes.kidName]. "How're you doing, little man?" [ynes.kidName] opens one eye to look at you, gasps, and waves his little hand at you. You let him grasp a finger, and pet his curly auburn hair. Ynes [ynes.numKids 1 2 3 |leans over you, marveling at your beautiful son. "Someone's happy to see his [pc.mf |daddy |mommy ]." |goes to your second-born, plucking up a bundle of burbling babe from the cradle. "Oh, here we go. Mommy's little menaces. Happy to see their [pc.mf |dad |mom ], aren't you?" |goes to the cribs huddled by the fireplace, inspecting your younger children. She lifts one into her arms, waving the babe's hand at you. "Oh, here we go. Mommy's little menaces. Happy to see their [pc.mf |dad |mom ], aren't you?" ] "Time for me to go get some tea," the cowgirl nursemaid says, handing [ynes.kidName] to you. "Call when you need me." You and Ynes take a seat on the couch, [ynes.numKids 1 2 |fawning over [ynes.kidName]. |each with a baby in your arms ]. "Now this is nice," your lover smiles, ticking [ynes.kidName] on his cheek. "It still feels a little unreal to me, you know. Having [ynes.numKids 1 2 |a son |a family like this ]. A road closed, suddenly opened. And it feels so... good. Fulfilling, in a way I never imagined. Just look at [ynes.kidName]! My handsome little man. I can't stop thinking about what kind of a person he's going to grow up to be. Will he be [pc.dcbc |a ruthless anti-hero |a knight in shining armor |a dim-witted, loveable fool |a smarmy little bastard ] like his [pc.mf |father |mother ], or..." You bump your shoulder against hers. "He's going to be great.[ynes.numKids 2 3 | They both are. | They all are. ]" Ynes draws in a deep, unsteady breath. "I, yes, you're right. Particularly when [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he has |they have ] such a good role model.[pc.isDK | In Eirina. And the Astrida. And everybody here who isn't us. ]" Your lover sighs and rests her head on your shoulder. "I can only try not to drive myself mad with worry. But it isn't easy." [pc.isDK |"Hey, I say let [ynes.numKids 1 2 |him |them ] do what [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he wants |they want ]!" you say, ruffling [ynes.kidName]'s hair. He fusses, swatting at your finger. "With how powerful you were, and I am... [ynes.kidName] could take over the damn world, I bet. If I don't get there first." Ynes shoots you a look. "Careful how you speak here! Though... that does have a certain appeal, doesn't it? Mothering the warlock who'll bring order back to a world in chaos..." Yeah it does... | "Hey, you're in the best place for it, at least," you say, kissing the top of her head. "Nareva's faithful won't let [ynes.numKids 1 2 |him |them ] go astray." "One hopes," Ynes says. "But I will do my part, too. Let our children learn from my mistakes..." You hold her close. "You're a great mom, Ynes. You've got this." ] You stay a while longer, just enjoying the closeness with your partner and your [ynes.numKids 1 2 |son |children ]. It's not long before [ynes.kidName] is fast asleep in your arms, drooling down his chin. Ynes smirks and takes him, putting him into his crib by the fireplace. "A moment without him whining for milk is a rare treat. Come, let's allow [ynes.kidName] his rest." You don't let the witch lead you out before pulling her down into your lap and giving her a kiss first, at least. Only then do you let her take your hand and make your way back up to the temple, passing by the cowgirl enjoying her tea break on your way.
rows.translation: 你俯身越过图书管理员的办公桌，往下面窥视。“它在哪儿？ [ynes.kidName]伊内斯翻了个白眼：“真不想跟你说这个，但教士们对在圣所里听到婴儿哭声可没什么好感。 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] 是 |我们的孩子们 ] 在楼下，和其他一些“无梦者”们在一起。我们当中最善良的人之一，莱娜，是科尔米诺人——所以牛奶的问题根本不用担心。而我也会每隔一小时左右偷偷溜出去看看她。别担心。 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] 是 |我们的孩子们 ] 照顾得真好。” “我们现在就能去看看吗？” 伊内斯宠溺地看了你一眼，然后从办公桌后面站了起来：“哦，好吧，跟我来吧。我肯定…… [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |孩子们 ] 非常高兴见到你。” 你帮助伊内斯站了起来，然后由她带领你前往寺庙下方的仆人居住区。这里的环境虽然简陋，但得益于那些反映了“无梦之地”各地区文化的编织挂毯和地毯，尽管墙壁是由石头砌成的，这里依然温暖舒适：你可以看到霍尔米诺斯地区的几何图案，贝尔哈尔南部地区的鲜艳色彩，寒冷北方地区的冰蓝色调，甚至还有来自沿海地区的鱼与火主题图案。 下面还有其他孩子们在走廊里奔跑、欢笑——但这些孩子并不是“梦无族”的成员。这些是年轻的阿斯特里达，它们是身披黑色鳞片、身上带有天蓝色与深红色斑纹的蝾螈，身上还戴着装饰着银色花纹的小型格子围裙。 [var0|想到有那么一天自己的孩子也会成为他们中的一员，你不禁会露出微笑。|它们年纪有点大了，不适合再用来玩耍了。 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |你的孩子们 ]不过，也是这样。] 你从它们身边走过，拨开它们乱乱的毛发，拍打那些乱动的尾巴，直到来到最深处那扇被涂成黑色的门前。 “我们到了，”伊内斯说着，走了进去。“就在那里。 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |他是 |它们是 ]里面是一个布置简单的客厅，客厅的中心是一处壁炉和烟囱。一位牛仔姑娘正坐在那里，她把你的儿子抱在腿上，面带微笑地给这个饥饿的小家伙喂奶。“哦，伊内斯！冠军也在这里呢！你好。” 你挥了挥手，然后跪下来想仔细看看…… [ynes.kidName]小家伙，最近过得怎么样？ [ynes.kidName] 他睁开一只眼睛看着你，倒吸了一口气，然后用他的小手朝你挥了挥。你让他握住你的手指，然后轻轻抚摸他那卷曲的浅棕色头发。伊内斯 [ynes.numKids 1 2 3 |她俯身看着你，为你漂亮的儿子感到惊叹。“有人见到自己的孩子真是太开心了…… [pc.mf |爸爸 |妈妈 ]." |然后把第二个孩子从婴儿床里抱起来，那个婴儿正发出咯咯的笑声。“哦，来了。妈妈的小捣蛋鬼们，见到你们真开心！ [pc.mf |爸爸 |妈妈 ]你不是吗？ |她走到壁炉旁那些婴儿床前，检查你们年幼的孩子们。她抱起其中一个，还让婴儿朝你挥了挥手。“哦，来了。妈妈的小捣蛋鬼们，见到你们真开心。 [pc.mf |爸爸 |妈妈 ]你不是吗？ ] 该我去泡点茶了，”那位穿着牛仔装的女护士说道，一边说着一边递上了东西。 [ynes.kidName] 有需要的话随时叫我。” 你和伊内斯坐在沙发上， [ynes.numKids 1 2 |阿谀奉承 [ynes.kidName]. |每个人都怀抱着自己的孩子 ]现在可真是太好了，”你的爱人微笑着说道。 [ynes.kidName] 他摸了摸自己的脸颊，说道：“你知道，对我来说，这一切仍然感觉有点不真实。毕竟…… [ynes.numKids 1 2 |儿子 |这样的家庭 ]一条原本封闭的道路，突然重新打开了。这种感觉真是……太好了。在某种意义上，这种满足感完全超出了我的想象。只要看看就行了。 [ynes.kidName]! 我这个帅气的小家伙……我总是忍不住想象他长大后会成为什么样的人。他会是一个…… [pc.dcbc |一个冷酷无情的反英雄 |一位身着闪亮盔甲的骑士 |一个愚蠢却惹人喜爱的傻瓜 |一个油嘴滑舌的小混蛋 ] 和他一样 [pc.mf |父亲 |母亲 ]或者……” 你的肩膀不小心撞到了她的肩膀。“他会变得很出色的。[ynes.numKids 2 3 | 他们两个都是。 | 他们都是。 ]伊内斯深吸了一口气，但气息并不平稳。“是的，你说得对。尤其是在那种情况下…… [ynes.numKids 1 2 |他有 |他们拥有 ] 真是个很好的榜样。[pc.isDK | 在艾琳娜，还有阿斯特里达，以及这里所有不是我们的人。 ]你的爱人轻叹着，将头靠在你的肩上。‘我只能…… 尝试一下吧。 我可不想因为担心而把自己逼疯，但这确实不容易。 [pc.isDK |嘿，我觉得我们应该…… [ynes.numKids 1 2 |他 |他们 ] 做某事 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |他想要 |他们想要 ]你说得对，”他说道，语气中带着一丝恼怒。 [ynes.kidName]他的头发。他不停地扭动，还试图拍打你的手指。“以你的实力，我…… 是... [ynes.kidName] 我敢打赌，就算要统治整个世界我也完全有能力。只要我能先做到这一点就行。” 伊内斯瞥了你一眼：“在这里说话可要小心点！不过……这种想法确实有点吸引力，不是吗？照顾那个能够让混乱的世界恢复秩序的术士……” 是啊，确实很有吸引力…… | 嘿，至少你现在处于最有利的位置，”你说道，同时轻吻她的头顶。“纳雷瓦的忠实追随者们不会让…… [ynes.numKids 1 2 |他 |他们 ] 但愿如此，”伊内斯说道，“不过我也会尽自己的一份力。让我们的孩子从我的错误中吸取教训吧……” 你紧紧地将她抱在怀里：“你是一位了不起的母亲，伊内斯。你一定能够做到的。 ] 再多待一会儿吧，好好享受与伴侣在一起的时光，享受这份亲密感。 [ynes.numKids 1 2 |儿子 |孩子们 ]没过多久， [ynes.kidName] 他正在你的怀里熟睡，口水都流到了下巴上。伊内斯笑着把他抱起来，放在壁炉旁的婴儿床里。“能让他安静一会儿，不用一直哭着要喝奶，真是难得的享受。来吧，让我们也让他享受一下这样的时刻吧。 [ynes.kidName] 让他安静地休息吧。” 至少，在把那个女巫拉到自己怀里亲她一下之前，你不会让她先带你出去。只有在那之后，你才会让她牵着你的手，一起回到神庙。在回去的路上，你会经过那个正在享受茶歇时间的牛仔姑娘。
rows.raw: ' You lean over the librarian's desk, peering underneath it. "Where's [ynes.kidName]?" Ynes rolls her eyes. "I hate to break it to you, but the clergy take a dim view of babies wailing in the sanctuary. [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] is |Our children are ] downstairs, with some of the other Dreamless. One of the most harmless of us all, Lenna, is a Khor'minoan — so milk is taken care of. And I manage to slip away to visit every hour or so. Don't worry. [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] is |Our children are ] well taken care of." "Can we go and visit right now?" Ynes gives you an indulgent look and pushes back from her desk. "Oh, very well, come along. I'm sure [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |the children ] will be happy to see you." You help Ynes up, and let her lead the way down to the servants' quarters underneath the temple. It's spartan down here, but warm despite the walls of stone thanks to woven tapestries and rugs reflecting the cultures of the Dreamless down here: you recognize the geometric patterns of Khor'minos, the bright colors of southern Belhar, icy hues of the cold north, and even fish-and-fire themes from the coastal realms. There are other children down here, too, running down down the halls and laughing — but these aren't the Dreamless's get. These are young Astrida, black-scaled salamanders with accents of azure and crimson, wearing little tartan togas adorned with silver oroboroses. [var0|You can't help but smile, knowing that someday your own children will be among them.|They're a bit too old to be playing with [ynes.numKids 1 2 |[ynes.kidName] |your children ], though.] You pass them by, ruffling hair and swatting at errant tails, until you make your way to a black-painted door in the depths. "Here we are," Ynes says, heading in. "And there [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he is |they are ]." Inside is a simply-furnished sitting room built around a fireplace and chimney, in which a cowgirl's sitting with your son in her lap, nursing the hungry little lad with a smile on her face. "Oh, Ynes! And the Champion as well! Hello." You wave, kneeling down to get a peek at [ynes.kidName]. "How're you doing, little man?" [ynes.kidName] opens one eye to look at you, gasps, and waves his little hand at you. You let him grasp a finger, and pet his curly auburn hair. Ynes [ynes.numKids 1 2 3 |leans over you, marveling at your beautiful son. "Someone's happy to see his [pc.mf |daddy |mommy ]." |goes to your second-born, plucking up a bundle of burbling babe from the cradle. "Oh, here we go. Mommy's little menaces. Happy to see their [pc.mf |dad |mom ], aren't you?" |goes to the cribs huddled by the fireplace, inspecting your younger children. She lifts one into her arms, waving the babe's hand at you. "Oh, here we go. Mommy's little menaces. Happy to see their [pc.mf |dad |mom ], aren't you?" ] "Time for me to go get some tea," the cowgirl nursemaid says, handing [ynes.kidName] to you. "Call when you need me." You and Ynes take a seat on the couch, [ynes.numKids 1 2 |fawning over [ynes.kidName]. |each with a baby in your arms ]. "Now this is nice," your lover smiles, ticking [ynes.kidName] on his cheek. "It still feels a little unreal to me, you know. Having [ynes.numKids 1 2 |a son |a family like this ]. A road closed, suddenly opened. And it feels so... good. Fulfilling, in a way I never imagined. Just look at [ynes.kidName]! My handsome little man. I can't stop thinking about what kind of a person he's going to grow up to be. Will he be [pc.dcbc |a ruthless anti-hero |a knight in shining armor |a dim-witted, loveable fool |a smarmy little bastard ] like his [pc.mf |father |mother ], or..." You bump your shoulder against hers. "He's going to be great.[ynes.numKids 2 3 | They both are. | They all are. ]" Ynes draws in a deep, unsteady breath. "I, yes, you're right. Particularly when [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he has |they have ] such a good role model.[pc.isDK | In Eirina. And the Astrida. And everybody here who isn't us. ]" Your lover sighs and rests her head on your shoulder. "I can only try not to drive myself mad with worry. But it isn't easy." [pc.isDK |"Hey, I say let [ynes.numKids 1 2 |him |them ] do what [ynes.numKids 1 2 |he wants |they want ]!" you say, ruffling [ynes.kidName]'s hair. He fusses, swatting at your finger. "With how powerful you were, and I am... [ynes.kidName] could take over the damn world, I bet. If I don't get there first." Ynes shoots you a look. "Careful how you speak here! Though... that does have a certain appeal, doesn't it? Mothering the warlock who'll bring order back to a world in chaos..." Yeah it does... | "Hey, you're in the best place for it, at least," you say, kissing the top of her head. "Nareva's faithful won't let [ynes.numKids 1 2 |him |them ] go astray." "One hopes," Ynes says. "But I will do my part, too. Let our children learn from my mistakes..." You hold her close. "You're a great mom, Ynes. You've got this." ] You stay a while longer, just enjoying the closeness with your partner and your [ynes.numKids 1 2 |son |children ]. It's not long before [ynes.kidName] is fast asleep in your arms, drooling down his chin. Ynes smirks and takes him, putting him into his crib by the fireplace. "A moment without him whining for milk is a rare treat. Come, let's allow [ynes.kidName] his rest." You don't let the witch lead you out before pulling her down into your lap and giving her a kiss first, at least. Only then do you let her take your hand and make your way back up to the temple, passing by the cowgirl enjoying her tea break on your way. '

rows.translation: [ynes.numKids 2|孩子们|[ynes.kidName]]

rows.original: "Tell me about yourself." Ynes shifts uncomfortably. "I am a humble thrall of the temple, Champion. There's not much to tell." You gesture at the collar around her neck. "By thrall..." "I mean that I am bound in service here, and glad for it," Ynes says. "The temple has given me a better life than I deserve. My soul would have been damned long, long ago if not for the Order Astrida." "That sounds like there's something to tell." [var0|Ynes frowns. "I am not proud of the life I once lived, my Champion. I was a blackhearted villain, in thrall to a man even worse than I. We both sought power, and reveled in using it to the abuse of others. We delved deeply into the darkest reaches of magic, unbound by the gods' wisdom, and our souls paid the price — as did innocent people." "What do you mean?" "I had the misfortune to be born with a sharp mind, in a village full of peaceful dullards," Ynes says, fingers steepling. "Farmers in the gap between Zhenna Mountain and the coast, lorded over by a count who traced his line back to the old empire, and kept his Belharan pride. Tiburcio was his name.[pc.bg scholar arcanist |" Tiburcio of Corlund... you'd recognize that name anywhere! Your teacher would use him as an example of the fate that awaited those who misused sorcery: he fell to corruption, and used his sorcery to enslave his people, turning them into puppets on strings of magic. A Narevan mage-hunter eventually uncovered his dark work and broke his spells, then dragged him before the village and burned him. Grim business. " | ]He recognized my talent as a youth and bought me from my parents. My life then was worth five hundred hawks: a princely sum to poor farmers. I served in his estate as a maidservant for a few years, under the strict supervision of an elven matron that had served him all his life... and who was canny enough to recognize that my talents were better spent. She taught me to read in secret, then bid me demonstrate my talents. Even then Tiburcio was cruel and hard-handed, but he was not wasteful. He elevated me. Taught me himself. But I wasn't an apprentice, not really — he certainly never respected me like a true student. If he had, perhaps he might have listened..." "What happened?" "My master became obsessed with ruins beneath his ancestral estate. Brought in swarthy workmen and scholars from abroad to excavate them. It cost him his fortune, but he was right, in his way. There were ancient relics hidden there in the dark. A causeway connecting his estate to a sanctum that had once belonged to his magisterial ancestors. And something... alive. I still don't understand it, not fully, but something writhed in the dark down there. An echo of the Godswar, I think. It pierced my master's mind, and filled him with power. By the time we had reached that sanctum, we had shed much of our equipment — our spellbooks among them — in order to squeeze through the long, constrained passage, but Tiburcio was overcome with a mad passion, a need to write down what had filled his mind before it left again. We had no ink with us, no paper. But my master had a knife, and I had flesh to spare." Ynes shudders at the memory, fingers brushing the hem of her collar. "From that moment on, I was his spellbook. And I was never far from his side, even as he delved into the forbidden depths of magic... even as he bent the minds of his household, and then all the village, to worship him like a god. And, to them, I was his apostle. The voice of his will. Glowing with his power. We lived like a king and his queen, until Eirina came." You can tell that the further she goes in this story, the less comfortable she gets... but at this point, she might as well continue. You gesture for her to go on. "As I said, I am not proud of what I became, but in the moment, it seemed only natural to exult in the power we had unearthed. We reveled in debauchery, abused those around us like they were chattel. My body became a temple to dark magic etched in blood and arcane inks. I am glad I cannot dream, because I have no doubt that if I could, I would never sleep again. I thank the goddess she sent one of her servants to us — she stumbled upon our village and immediately caught onto our spells ensorcelling the people there." "As powerful as we thought we were, we were nothing compared to the might of an Astrida. She carved through our spells with ease, every charm and illusion, even the darkest blood magics we had mastered barely slowed her. My master and I were as helpless as babes before a dragon. Each spell we cast against her only made her stronger! We'd lost before the battle even began." "It sounds like you're lucky to be alive," you say. Ynes shakes her head. "I don't know about that. I'm glad I am. Eirina insists that I was a victim, too. That my master's magic had twisted my mind, too. But I don't really believe that. I can't. I remember everything I did. Every thought I had. The horrible things I said, and felt, and did." She shivers. "I'm sorry, I... like I said, I am glad to now be a slave to the temple. To be Dreamless. Eirina did me a kindness in sparing me. Without Tiburcio I would have labored as a peasant on the land, now I have the privilege of working here in comfort and quiet. It suits me. It's better than I deserve."|The redhead gives you a narrow-eyed look. "Not to you, there is not. The goddess does love a good mystery, doesn't she?"]
rows.translation: 请介绍一下你自己吧。” 伊内斯有些不自在地挪了挪身子。“我只是这座神庙里一个卑微的仆从罢了，冠军。没什么可说的。” 你指了指她脖子上的项圈。“你说的‘仆从’……” “我的意思是，我在这里承担着服务职责，而且对此感到庆幸，”伊内斯说道，“神庙给了我远超我应得的美好生活。如果没有阿斯特里达教团，我的灵魂早就堕入地狱了。” “那…… 听起来不错。 好像有什么事情要告诉大家似的。 [var0|伊内斯皱起了眉头：“我对自己曾经过的生活并不感到自豪，我的勇士。我曾是一个心肠歹毒的恶人，受制于一个比我还恶劣的人。我们俩都追逐权力，并且乐于用权力去伤害他人。我们深入探索了魔法中最黑暗的领域，无视神明的教诲，最终我们的灵魂为此付出了代价——无辜的人们也同样受到了伤害。你是什么意思？” “我真是不幸，生在一个充满愚钝之人的村庄里，却天生头脑敏锐，”伊内斯说道，手指交叉着。“我们生活在詹娜山与海岸之间的这片土地上，这里的农民们生活在一位可以追溯到古老帝国的伯爵的统治之下，而这位伯爵依然保持着贝拉兰人的骄傲与尊严。 他的名字叫蒂布里奥。[pc.bg scholar arcanist |科伦德的蒂布尔西奥……这个名字在任何地方你都能认出来！你的老师一定会用他来举例，说明那些滥用魔法的人会面临怎样的命运：他堕落了，用魔法奴役自己的族人，把他们变成了被魔法控制的傀儡。最终，一位纳雷万的法师猎手揭露了他的邪恶行径，破解了他的魔法，然后把他拖到村子里烧死了。真是残酷的结局啊…… | ]他很早就看中了我的才华，于是从我父母手中买下了我。在那个时候，我的价值相当于五百只鹰——对那些贫困的农民来说，这是一笔巨额的财富。我在他的庄园里当了几年的女仆，期间一直受到一位为他还服侍了一辈子的精灵女管家的严格监管。还有一个人，她足够精明，能够意识到我的才能应该被用在更值得的地方。她暗中教我读书，然后让我展示自己的能力。即便如此，蒂布西奥依然残忍而严厉，但他并没有浪费我的才华——他让我得到了晋升。 是他亲自教我的。但我其实并不是他的学徒——他从来都没有把我当作真正的学生来对待。如果他真的这么做了，或许就会听我的意见了…… “发生了什么事？” “我的师父对他祖宅地下的那些遗迹产生了浓厚的兴趣，甚至对此着了迷。 他从国外招募了肤色黝黑的工人和学者来挖掘这些遗迹。这耗尽了他的全部财产，但从某种意义上说，他确实是正确的。因为在那些黑暗的深处，确实隐藏着古老的遗物——一条连接他的庄园与一座曾经属于他祖先的圣地的通道。 还有某种……有生命的东西。我至今仍未完全理解它，但…… 某个东西 在黑暗中扭曲着，我想那是“神战”留下的回响。那种力量穿透了我主人的心灵，赋予了他强大的力量。当我们抵达那个神圣之地时，为了穿过那些狭窄而漫长的通道，我们已经丢弃了许多装备——包括那些法术书——但蒂布尔西奥被一种疯狂的冲动所驱使，他必须把那些涌入他脑海中的信息记录下来，否则它们就会再次消失无踪。 我们身上没有墨水，也没有纸张。但我的主人有一把刀，而我的身上还有多余的肉。 想到这里，伊内斯不寒而栗，手指轻轻触碰着自己的衣领。“从那一刻起…… 我 那是他的法术书。而我一直陪伴在他身边，无论他深入探索魔法的禁秘领域……无论他如何影响他的家人，乃至整个村庄，让他们像崇拜神明一样崇拜他。对他们来说，我就是他的使徒，是他意志的代言人，承载着他的力量。我们过着国王与王后般的生活，直到艾琳娜的出现。 你可以看出，她在这个故事中越走越深，就越感到不安……但在这种情况下，她或许还是应该继续下去。你示意她继续说下去。 “正如我所说，我对自己现在的样子并不感到自豪，但在那一刻，能够为我们所拥有的力量而欢欣鼓舞，似乎是再自然不过的事情了。 我们沉溺于放纵之中，将周围的人当作物品一样肆意虐待。我的身体变成了一个承载着黑暗魔法、用鲜血与神秘墨水铭刻着符文的殿堂。我很庆幸自己无法做梦，因为我相信，如果我能做梦的话，我就再也无法入睡了。 我感谢那位女神，她派了她的一个仆从来到我们这里——那个仆从偶然发现了我们的村庄，立刻就察觉到了我们用来迷惑那里人们的魔法。 尽管我们自认为实力不俗，但与阿斯特里达的力量相比，我们简直微不足道。她轻而易举地破解了我们的所有法术——无论是那些咒语还是幻术，甚至是我们掌握的最强大的血魔法，也几乎无法对她产生任何影响。我和我的师父，在她面前就像婴儿面对巨龙一样无助。我们对她施展的每一个法术，反而只是让她的力量变得更加强大！实际上，在这场战斗开始之前，我们就已经输掉了。 你能活下来，确实算是运气好。”你说道。 伊内斯摇了摇头：“我也不确定。我只是很高兴自己还活着。艾琳娜坚持认为，我也是受害者之一，认为我的主人的魔法也扭曲了我的思想。但我并不真的相信这一点。 我做不到。我记得自己做过的每一件事，每一个念头。那些我说过、感受过、做过的一切可怕的事情……”她颤抖着说道，“对不起，我……就像我之前说的，现在能成为寺庙的奴隶，成为‘无梦者’，我反而感到庆幸。艾琳娜没有杀我，真是帮了我一个大忙。 如果没有蒂布西奥，我就会在田地里当一个农民辛苦劳作，而现在，我有幸能够在这里舒适而安静地工作。这种生活很适合我，甚至比我应得的还要好。|那个红发女子用眯起的眼睛看着你：“不是说给你听的，其实并没有。女神就是喜欢一些神秘的情节，对吧？]
rows.raw: ' "Tell me about yourself." Ynes shifts uncomfortably. "I am a humble thrall of the temple, Champion. There's not much to tell." You gesture at the collar around her neck. "By thrall..." "I mean that I am bound in service here, and glad for it," Ynes says. "The temple has given me a better life than I deserve. My soul would have been damned long, long ago if not for the Order Astrida." "That sounds like there's something to tell." [var0|Ynes frowns. "I am not proud of the life I once lived, my Champion. I was a blackhearted villain, in thrall to a man even worse than I. We both sought power, and reveled in using it to the abuse of others. We delved deeply into the darkest reaches of magic, unbound by the gods' wisdom, and our souls paid the price — as did innocent people." "What do you mean?" "I had the misfortune to be born with a sharp mind, in a village full of peaceful dullards," Ynes says, fingers steepling. "Farmers in the gap between Zhenna Mountain and the coast, lorded over by a count who traced his line back to the old empire, and kept his Belharan pride. Tiburcio was his name.[pc.bg scholar arcanist |" Tiburcio of Corlund... you'd recognize that name anywhere! Your teacher would use him as an example of the fate that awaited those who misused sorcery: he fell to corruption, and used his sorcery to enslave his people, turning them into puppets on strings of magic. A Narevan mage-hunter eventually uncovered his dark work and broke his spells, then dragged him before the village and burned him. Grim business. " | ]He recognized my talent as a youth and bought me from my parents. My life then was worth five hundred hawks: a princely sum to poor farmers. I served in his estate as a maidservant for a few years, under the strict supervision of an elven matron that had served him all his life... and who was canny enough to recognize that my talents were better spent. She taught me to read in secret, then bid me demonstrate my talents. Even then Tiburcio was cruel and hard-handed, but he was not wasteful. He elevated me. Taught me himself. But I wasn't an apprentice, not really — he certainly never respected me like a true student. If he had, perhaps he might have listened..." "What happened?" "My master became obsessed with ruins beneath his ancestral estate. Brought in swarthy workmen and scholars from abroad to excavate them. It cost him his fortune, but he was right, in his way. There were ancient relics hidden there in the dark. A causeway connecting his estate to a sanctum that had once belonged to his magisterial ancestors. And something... alive. I still don't understand it, not fully, but something writhed in the dark down there. An echo of the Godswar, I think. It pierced my master's mind, and filled him with power. By the time we had reached that sanctum, we had shed much of our equipment — our spellbooks among them — in order to squeeze through the long, constrained passage, but Tiburcio was overcome with a mad passion, a need to write down what had filled his mind before it left again. We had no ink with us, no paper. But my master had a knife, and I had flesh to spare." Ynes shudders at the memory, fingers brushing the hem of her collar. "From that moment on, I was his spellbook. And I was never far from his side, even as he delved into the forbidden depths of magic... even as he bent the minds of his household, and then all the village, to worship him like a god. And, to them, I was his apostle. The voice of his will. Glowing with his power. We lived like a king and his queen, until Eirina came." You can tell that the further she goes in this story, the less comfortable she gets... but at this point, she might as well continue. You gesture for her to go on. "As I said, I am not proud of what I became, but in the moment, it seemed only natural to exult in the power we had unearthed. We reveled in debauchery, abused those around us like they were chattel. My body became a temple to dark magic etched in blood and arcane inks. I am glad I cannot dream, because I have no doubt that if I could, I would never sleep again. I thank the goddess she sent one of her servants to us — she stumbled upon our village and immediately caught onto our spells ensorcelling the people there." "As powerful as we thought we were, we were nothing compared to the might of an Astrida. She carved through our spells with ease, every charm and illusion, even the darkest blood magics we had mastered barely slowed her. My master and I were as helpless as babes before a dragon. Each spell we cast against her only made her stronger! We'd lost before the battle even began." "It sounds like you're lucky to be alive," you say. Ynes shakes her head. "I don't know about that. I'm glad I am. Eirina insists that I was a victim, too. That my master's magic had twisted my mind, too. But I don't really believe that. I can't. I remember everything I did. Every thought I had. The horrible things I said, and felt, and did." She shivers. "I'm sorry, I... like I said, I am glad to now be a slave to the temple. To be Dreamless. Eirina did me a kindness in sparing me. Without Tiburcio I would have labored as a peasant on the land, now I have the privilege of working here in comfort and quiet. It suits me. It's better than I deserve."|The redhead gives you a narrow-eyed look. "Not to you, there is not. The goddess does love a good mystery, doesn't she?"]'

rows.original: "You stand in the grand hall of Nareva herself," Ynes says, a hint of pride in her voice. "There is no greater house of learning anywhere on the continent. Here, wizards and scholars from every domain of Old Belhar come to share in the knowledge housed under the goddess's roof." "Can anybody just come and study here?" "Yes, though much of what is here remains under lock and key to the unworthy. Nareva is also the goddess of secrets, and knowing with whom knowledge can be safely shared in as much a pillar of her faith as the learning itself — and so is the thrill of earning that right, that trust, to unlock her inner mysteries." In other words, it's not suspicious for anybody from anywhere to be here in the temple. "Does the temple ever turn anybody away?" That quest makes Ynes pause for thought. "I haven't seen it happen in years, but occasionally someone will come with ill intent in their heart, hoping to take advantage of the goddess's house. Thankfully [pc.ra astrida |the Order Astrida are |your order is ] talented as sniffing such ne'er-do-wells out. A good way to be made Dreamless, like me." "Is there anything on the grounds other than the library?" you ask. "[pc.ra astrida |You would know better than I!" Ynes laughs. "You have seen more of the temple's underground labyrinth than I ever will. |The garrison for the Order Astrida, and a complex of laboratories and cells for magical experimentation. Beneath the temple is a deep labyrinth of chambers that I have never seen — few have, save the Astrida and the temple's high clerics. ] I understand the excavation was a feat of engineering to rival the tunnels of Khor'minos, though I suspect that may be some exaggeration..." [pc.ra astrida |Hardly. |That does sound unlikely... ]
rows.translation: 你现在正站在纳雷瓦女神的宏伟殿堂之中，”伊内斯说道，她的语气中带着一丝自豪。“在整个大陆上，没有比这里更伟大的学术殿堂了。在这里，来自古老贝拉尔各个领域的巫师和学者们齐聚一堂，共同分享蕴藏在这位女神庇护之下的知识。有没有人能来这里学习呢？” “可以，不过这里很多知识都对那些不配学习的人关闭着。纳雷瓦同时也是秘密的女神，知道该与谁安全地分享知识，这一点与学习本身一样，都是她信仰的重要支柱——而获得这种权利、赢得这种信任，从而揭开她内心的秘密，所带来的兴奋感，也同样重要。换句话说， 对于任何来自任何地方的人来说，来到这座寺庙里，并没有什么可疑之处。这座神庙会拒绝任何人吗？” 这个问题让伊内斯陷入了沉思。“我已经很多年没有见过这种情况发生了，但偶尔也会有人怀着不良的意图来到这里，试图利用这位女神的神殿。所幸，这样的情况并不多见。 [pc.ra astrida |阿斯特里达骑士团 |您的订单已处理完毕。 ] 拥有这样的天赋，就能轻易识破那些无能之辈。这正是像我一样，最终陷入‘无梦’状态的最佳途径。” “除了图书馆，这个地方还有其他什么吗？”你问道。[pc.ra astrida |你肯定比我更清楚！”伊内斯笑道，“你见过这座寺庙地下迷宫的更多地方，而这些地方是我永远也不会有机会看到的。 |这是阿斯特里达教团的驻军基地，同时也是一系列用于魔法实验的实验室和试验室的所在地。在寺庙的地下，还有一片我从未见过的复杂迷宫般的通道——除了阿斯特里达教团的人以及寺庙的高阶神职人员，几乎没有人知道它的存在。 ] 我明白，进行这样的挖掘工程确实是一项堪比建造霍尔米诺斯隧道的伟大工程壮举，不过我怀疑这种说法可能有些夸张了…… [pc.ra astrida |不可能。 |那确实听起来不太可能…… ]
rows.raw: ' "You stand in the grand hall of Nareva herself," Ynes says, a hint of pride in her voice. "There is no greater house of learning anywhere on the continent. Here, wizards and scholars from every domain of Old Belhar come to share in the knowledge housed under the goddess's roof." "Can anybody just come and study here?" "Yes, though much of what is here remains under lock and key to the unworthy. Nareva is also the goddess of secrets, and knowing with whom knowledge can be safely shared in as much a pillar of her faith as the learning itself — and so is the thrill of earning that right, that trust, to unlock her inner mysteries." In other words, it's not suspicious for anybody from anywhere to be here in the temple. "Does the temple ever turn anybody away?" That quest makes Ynes pause for thought. "I haven't seen it happen in years, but occasionally someone will come with ill intent in their heart, hoping to take advantage of the goddess's house. Thankfully [pc.ra astrida |the Order Astrida are |your order is ] talented as sniffing such ne'er-do-wells out. A good way to be made Dreamless, like me." "Is there anything on the grounds other than the library?" you ask. "[pc.ra astrida |You would know better than I!" Ynes laughs. "You have seen more of the temple's underground labyrinth than I ever will. |The garrison for the Order Astrida, and a complex of laboratories and cells for magical experimentation. Beneath the temple is a deep labyrinth of chambers that I have never seen — few have, save the Astrida and the temple's high clerics. ] I understand the excavation was a feat of engineering to rival the tunnels of Khor'minos, though I suspect that may be some exaggeration..." [pc.ra astrida |Hardly. |That does sound unlikely... ] '

rows.translation: 神庙

rows.original: [pc.ra astrida |"You of all people should know!" Ynes laughs. "It is within your power to inflict this state upon others, my [pc.mf |lord |lady ]." "I mean, what does it mean to you, Ynes?" The beautiful thrall sighs. "A lifetime of peace, and some small reparation for what I did. I deserved far worse than what Eirina did to me, [pc.name]. I have no illusions, with the benefit of detached perspective, that I was anything but a great evil in the world. And still, Eirina showed me mercy. I will forever be in her debt for that. A moment of soul-deep agony exchanged for a path to the goddess's forgiveness..." She allows herself a small smile, and rubs at her eyes. | "Ah, of course... I doubt you've had reason to fear the Order, [pc.isMage |being a Champion of the world |as you are not a mage by trade ]. But to be made Dreamless is the greatest punishment Nareva's godsworn blades can lay down — at least, that is the common perception. Having experienced it, I am grateful for what was done to me. Far better than a torturous end, or a life filled with unending nightmares." "So what actually is being Dreamless?" Ynes gives you a faint smile. "What does it sound like? From what I understand, there is an indelible connection between magic and the realm of dreams. One feeds the other. Astrida are magic-eaters, and they have the power to drain the magic from someone so completely, so utterly, that it severs them from the dreamscape entirely. A sorcerer subjected to this power will never be able to cast even the simplest cantrip again, and sleep will bring with it only a blackness, a blink that lasts for hours without a thought in your mind." "And they did that to you?" She nods. "It was Eirina herself, First Spear of the Order. I was a malefactor of great power, yet she easily overpowered me. Rather than burning me at the stake as I deserved, though, she bound me as her thrall and drew out every last drop of magic from my veins. I have served her ever since... and I am grateful for her mercy. To be Dreamless is not the horror it's oft described as, but a blessing. I live without temptation, without the torment of nightmares. I cannot regress into the witch I once was. I am unburdened." [pc.isDK |Sounds like cope to you. They ripped her power away and made her grateful for it? Hells. |She sounds like she's talking to herself more than you, now... ] "What was it like, having your power taken?" "It was..." Ynes rubs a thumb across her chin, eyes closing. "It was like feeling a hand reach in and grip my heart. It wasn't painful, not physically, but it was intense, overwhelming and exhausting. I screamed until my throat ran hoarse, I remember that much. You can't imagine... the very hand of the goddess reaching out and squeezing the magic from my soul." Ynes shivers. "But it was a process of minutes exchanged for a lifetime of peace. I would make that bargain again, were it my choice." ] "Are there many other Dreamless here?" "A few other servants of the temple, yes, though none work here in the front. Most of them were young mages, unable to control their magic, who became a danger to their communities and faced expulsion or death until the Order took them in. My breadth of experience and knowledge permits me some greater responsibilities. Most Dreamless are able to return to their lives, if they choose; those made thralls of the temple had nowhere else to go, or in my case, were deemed still too dangerous to release." [ynes.pregStage 2 |You lean across the desk and put a hand on Ynes's stomach. "Will your being Dreamless have any effect on our little one?" [ynes.numKids 0 1 |The former witch squirms a little in her seat. "I... truly do not know. None of the other Dreamless here have ever had children, but... I have never heard of any such thing. This is a neutering of my soul, I don't see why it would affect our child's." "So this is uncharted territory?" "I am sure other Dreamless over the centuries must have borne children. I can't be the first. And surely... surely the Order would not have approved of our union, if the children it produced would be mangled by my condition." You glance from Ynes to the statue of Nareva looming over her. If the goddess of magic approves of sucking the magic out of others, you wonder what she would think about children born Dreamless... |Ynes puts a hand over yours. "It hasn't affected [ynes.kidName]. I know our son dreams; not a day goes by that I do not watch him sleep. I just... I almost wish it did. I fear that someday he'll inherit my power, and my... my poor judgement. I fear-" You brush your finger over Ynes's lips. "Shush. You're wonderful, Ynes. He'll be wonderful too." "You..." ] ]
rows.translation: [pc.ra astrida |你们这些人中最应该明白这一点！”伊内斯笑道，“有能力让别人陷入这种境地的人，正是你们。 [pc.mf |领主 |女士 ]我的意思是，这到底意味着什么？ 致您伊内斯？” 这位美丽的奴隶叹了口气：“我只想过上平静的生活，以及得到一些对我所做之事的补偿。我所受的惩罚本应该比艾琳娜对我做的那些事情还要严厉得多。 [pc.name]我并不抱有任何幻想，以一种客观的角度来看，我在这个世界上无疑是个巨大的邪恶存在。然而，艾琳娜还是宽恕了我。为此，我将永远感激她。那一刻刻骨铭心的痛苦，换来了获得女神宽恕的机会……”她微微一笑，然后揉了揉眼睛。 | 啊，当然……我想你应该没有理由害怕那个组织吧。 [pc.isMage |成为世界冠军 |因为你并非以法师为职业。 ]但是，被剥夺梦境才是纳雷瓦那些被神明诅咒的利刃所能施加的最严厉的惩罚——至少，这是人们的普遍看法。经历过这种惩罚之后，我反而对所发生的一切心存感激。比起痛苦的死亡，或者一辈子都被无尽的噩梦所困扰，这种惩罚要好得多。” “那么，实际上到底是什么呢？ 是 成为无梦之人，意味着什么？” 伊内斯对你微微一笑：“这听起来像是怎样的体验呢？据我所知，魔法与梦境世界之间存在着一种不可分割的联系——它们彼此相互影响。阿斯特里达族就是那些能够吸收魔力的存在，他们拥有将他人的魔力彻底吸干的能力，这种力量会让人彻底与梦境世界断绝联系。 任何拥有这种力量的巫师，都将再也无法施展最简单的戏法；而睡眠带来的，也只会是一片黑暗——那种持续数小时、让人脑海中毫无思绪的黑暗。 他们真的对你做了这种事？她点了点头。“就是艾琳娜本人，那个组织的第一枪手。我是个拥有强大力量的恶徒，但她却轻而易举地制服了我。然而，她并没有按照我应得的惩罚将我烧死，而是把我囚禁为她的奴仆，然后从我体内榨取了最后一滴魔法。 从那以后，我一直侍奉着她……我真的很感激她的宽恕。失去梦境其实并不像人们描述的那样可怕，而是一种福分。我不再受到诱惑的困扰，也不再遭受噩梦的折磨。我再也不会变回从前那个女巫了。 我是 无负担的." [pc.isDK |在你看来，这简直就是一种变相的折磨。他们剥夺了她的力量，却还让她对此心存感激？简直太荒谬了。 |现在看来，她似乎更像是在自言自语，而不是在和你说话…… ] 失去自己的力量，那种感觉是怎样的？” “那种感觉……”伊内斯用拇指轻轻摩挲着下巴，闭上了眼睛。“就好像有一只手伸进来，紧紧抓住了我的心。并不痛苦，至少从身体上来说并不痛苦，但那种感觉确实很难受。 激烈那种感觉简直令人难以承受、精疲力竭。我不停地尖叫，直到喉咙都沙哑了，我记得就是这么回事。你根本无法想象……仿佛是女神亲自伸出手来，将我灵魂中的魔力夺走了。 伊内斯不禁打了个寒颤：“但这是用几分钟的时间换取了一生的安宁。如果可以重新选择，我还会再次做出这样的交易。 ] 这里还有其他很多没有梦想的人吗？” “是的，还有几位寺庙的仆从，不过他们并没有在这里工作。其中大多数都是那些无法控制自己魔力的年轻法师，他们对自己所在的社区构成了威胁，因此面临着被驱逐或死亡的命运，直到这个组织收留了他们。 我的丰富经验与知识使我有能力承担更重要的责任。大多数“无梦者”如果愿意的话，都可以回到自己的生活中去；而那些被寺庙奴役的人，则没有其他去处——至于我，则被认为仍然太危险，无法被释放。" [ynes.pregStage 2 |你凑近桌子，将手放在伊妮斯的肚子上，问道：“你没有梦境这种状态，会对我们的孩子产生什么影响吗？ [ynes.numKids 0 1 |那位前女巫在座位上微微扭动了一下。“我……真的不知道。在这里的其他人中，从来没有谁生过孩子，但是……我也从未听说过有这种事情发生。这应该是一种绝育手术吧…… 我的 灵魂啊，我不明白这为什么会影响到我们的孩子。” “那么，这确实是一个未知的领域吗？” “我敢肯定，在几个世纪以来，其他‘无梦者’也应该生过孩子。我不可能是第一个这样的人。 而且，可以肯定的是……如果我们这样的结合所诞生的孩子会因为我的这种体质而受到伤害，那么秩序组织肯定不会批准我们的结合的。” 你的目光在伊内斯和矗立在她身后的娜雷瓦雕像之间徘徊。如果你认为魔法女神会认可从他人身上吸取魔法的行为，那么你不禁会想，她会对那些天生没有魔法能力的孩子作何评价。.. |伊内斯将她的手放在你的手上。“它并没有产生任何影响。 [ynes.kidName]我知道我们的儿子有着自己的梦想；没有一天我不看着他入睡。我只是……我甚至希望这一切真的发生。我害怕有一天他会继承我的力量，还有我那……糟糕的判断力。我真的很害怕……” 你用手指轻轻触碰着Ynes的嘴唇：“嘘，别说了。你真的很棒，Ynes。我们的儿子也会变得很棒的。” “你…… ] ]
rows.raw: ' [pc.ra astrida |"You of all people should know!" Ynes laughs. "It is within your power to inflict this state upon others, my [pc.mf |lord |lady ]." "I mean, what does it mean to you, Ynes?" The beautiful thrall sighs. "A lifetime of peace, and some small reparation for what I did. I deserved far worse than what Eirina did to me, [pc.name]. I have no illusions, with the benefit of detached perspective, that I was anything but a great evil in the world. And still, Eirina showed me mercy. I will forever be in her debt for that. A moment of soul-deep agony exchanged for a path to the goddess's forgiveness..." She allows herself a small smile, and rubs at her eyes. | "Ah, of course... I doubt you've had reason to fear the Order, [pc.isMage |being a Champion of the world |as you are not a mage by trade ]. But to be made Dreamless is the greatest punishment Nareva's godsworn blades can lay down — at least, that is the common perception. Having experienced it, I am grateful for what was done to me. Far better than a torturous end, or a life filled with unending nightmares." "So what actually is being Dreamless?" Ynes gives you a faint smile. "What does it sound like? From what I understand, there is an indelible connection between magic and the realm of dreams. One feeds the other. Astrida are magic-eaters, and they have the power to drain the magic from someone so completely, so utterly, that it severs them from the dreamscape entirely. A sorcerer subjected to this power will never be able to cast even the simplest cantrip again, and sleep will bring with it only a blackness, a blink that lasts for hours without a thought in your mind." "And they did that to you?" She nods. "It was Eirina herself, First Spear of the Order. I was a malefactor of great power, yet she easily overpowered me. Rather than burning me at the stake as I deserved, though, she bound me as her thrall and drew out every last drop of magic from my veins. I have served her ever since... and I am grateful for her mercy. To be Dreamless is not the horror it's oft described as, but a blessing. I live without temptation, without the torment of nightmares. I cannot regress into the witch I once was. I am unburdened." [pc.isDK |Sounds like cope to you. They ripped her power away and made her grateful for it? Hells. |She sounds like she's talking to herself more than you, now... ] "What was it like, having your power taken?" "It was..." Ynes rubs a thumb across her chin, eyes closing. "It was like feeling a hand reach in and grip my heart. It wasn't painful, not physically, but it was intense, overwhelming and exhausting. I screamed until my throat ran hoarse, I remember that much. You can't imagine... the very hand of the goddess reaching out and squeezing the magic from my soul." Ynes shivers. "But it was a process of minutes exchanged for a lifetime of peace. I would make that bargain again, were it my choice." ] "Are there many other Dreamless here?" "A few other servants of the temple, yes, though none work here in the front. Most of them were young mages, unable to control their magic, who became a danger to their communities and faced expulsion or death until the Order took them in. My breadth of experience and knowledge permits me some greater responsibilities. Most Dreamless are able to return to their lives, if they choose; those made thralls of the temple had nowhere else to go, or in my case, were deemed still too dangerous to release." [ynes.pregStage 2 |You lean across the desk and put a hand on Ynes's stomach. "Will your being Dreamless have any effect on our little one?" [ynes.numKids 0 1 |The former witch squirms a little in her seat. "I... truly do not know. None of the other Dreamless here have ever had children, but... I have never heard of any such thing. This is a neutering of my soul, I don't see why it would affect our child's." "So this is uncharted territory?" "I am sure other Dreamless over the centuries must have borne children. I can't be the first. And surely... surely the Order would not have approved of our union, if the children it produced would be mangled by my condition." You glance from Ynes to the statue of Nareva looming over her. If the goddess of magic approves of sucking the magic out of others, you wonder what she would think about children born Dreamless... |Ynes puts a hand over yours. "It hasn't affected [ynes.kidName]. I know our son dreams; not a day goes by that I do not watch him sleep. I just... I almost wish it did. I fear that someday he'll inherit my power, and my... my poor judgement. I fear-" You brush your finger over Ynes's lips. "Shush. You're wonderful, Ynes. He'll be wonderful too." "You..." ] ] '

rows.original: "This city is... strange," Ynes admits cautiously, glancing furtively behind you, making sure none of the other acolytes are within earshot. "I grew up amidst farmers and hunters. We supported a count, who lorded over us. Here, the land is divvied up and worked by many lords, who all give a share of what they take from their farmers to the city's lords, who use it to feed so, so many people that don't work the land. It's created dynamics I'm wholly unused to." "Oh?" Ynes gestures south, towards the city center. "There's an imbalance of power here. The city relies on the people outside it, and those people exert more and more influence. Rural lords demand more freedoms, and the consortium that process their food in turn extract more wealth... there is a complex interplay of politics here that goes way over even my head." "How tense are things actually? It seems peaceful here." "We're in the temple!" Ynes laughs. "There's hardly brawls in the street, but I feel a growing tension even here on the palace grounds between city-folk, nobles, and the rural landholders. Just last month a farmer was outside the palace gates shouting to anyone that would listen about how the millers' consortium was choking the life from the city, and that the princeps was doing nothing to protect the people from merchants' greed. I almost sympathized, until he threw manure at the guards who came to move him from the road." [pc.isCunning |So there's a growing tension between the commonfolk and the merchant class, is it? And it sounds like the royal family's caught in the middle. ] [pc.bg noblescion courtesan slumrat |And this is why [pc.bg noblescion |your family |the lords ] back home made sure the city-folk didn't get too wealthy. Letting them amass wealth just means they amass power, and that's a dangerous challenge to the lords' rule... ] Ynes shakes her head. "But if you ignore all that, the city's beautiful, and wealthy, and full of such a deep respect for learning, unlike anything I could have dreamed of. I do love it here, even setting my own circumstances aside." She rubs at her collar absent-mindedly. "For every rabble-rouser in the streets, there's three philosophers debating the origin of life or the movement of the heavens, or magicians displaying some new feat of arcane power. [ynes.isMom |There is nowhere in all of Old Belhar I would rather raise our family... |There's nowhere more exciting in the North. Maybe all of Old Belhar ]."
rows.translation: 这座城市……很奇怪，”伊内斯小心翼翼地承认道，她偷偷地瞥了你一眼，确保周围没有其他信徒在听。“我在农民和猎人的环境中长大，我们当时受一位伯爵的统治。而在这里，土地被分给了许多领主，他们从农民那里获取收益后，会把其中的一部分上缴给城市的统治者，而这些统治者则用这些收入来养活那些根本不从事农业劳动的人。这种社会结构完全超出了我的认知范围。 哦？” 伊内斯指向南边，朝着市中心的方向。“这里的权力结构失衡了。这座城市依赖着城外的居民，而这些居民的影响力正在逐渐增强。农村的领主们要求更多的自由，而负责加工他们农产品的企业集团则从中攫取了更多的财富……这里的政治关系错综复杂，甚至让我也难以理解。 实际情况到底有多紧张呢？这里看起来似乎很平静。” “我们现在是在寺庙里啊！”伊内斯笑着说，“街上的斗殴几乎闻所未闻，但即便在宫殿的这片区域内，我也感觉到城市居民、贵族以及乡下地主们之间的紧张气氛正在逐渐加剧。 就在上个月，有位农民站在宫殿门外，向任何愿意听他说话的人喊诉：那些磨坊主们组成的联盟正在扼杀这座城市的生机，而城主却毫无行动，未能保护民众免受商人们的剥削与贪婪的侵害。 我差点就同情他了，直到他向那些来把他从路上移开的守卫扔粪便。 [pc.isCunning |所以，是这样的…… 普通民众与商人阶层之间的紧张关系日益加剧是吗？听起来王室家族似乎被卷入了这场纷争之中。 ] [pc.bg noblescion courtesan slumrat |这就是原因所在。 [pc.bg noblescion |你的家人 |那些贵族们 ] 家乡的人们会确保城里的居民不会变得过于富有。如果让他们积累财富，就意味着他们会获得权力，而这对领主的统治来说无疑是一个危险的挑战…… ] 伊内斯摇了摇头：“但如果你忽略这一切，就会发现这座城市非常美丽、充满财富，而且人们对知识的尊重程度之深，是我做梦也想不到的。我真的很喜欢这里，甚至把我的个人处境也抛到了一边。”她心不在焉地揉了揉衣领，“在街上的每一个闹事者旁边，都有三位哲学家在讨论生命的起源或天体的运动规律，还有魔术师们在展示他们那些新奇的魔法技巧。 [ynes.isMom |在整个旧贝尔哈尔地区，没有哪个地方比这里更适合我抚养我们的家庭了…… |在北方，没有比这更令人兴奋的地方了。或许整个老贝尔哈尔都是如此。 ]."

rows.translation: 这座城市

rows.original: Ynes shakes her head. "I am a thrall of the temple, hardly in a position to trade words with the princeps or his family. Though they do visit us on occasion, it is hardly a spontaneous thing; a high cleric or Lady Eirina are always here to greet them and ensure their needs are met. [kythie.isCursed |At least, they were..." "Oh? Has something changed?" "I shouldn't gossip." You lean across the desk, [ynes.fucked |caressing Ynes's cheek. "Come on. What's the harm?" |giving her your most reassuring smile. "I won't tell." ] Ynes sighs. "Well, it is not as though I was told to keep quiet, but..." She lowers her voice a little. "The palace has been sending messengers to the temple nearly every day, and Eirina has been turning them away. She's been buried in her books and refuses to be disturbed, even by the princeps. Many of the other Astrida are in much the same state, and members of the Order have been recalled from abroad to join them. It is concerning. I fear something dreadful must be on the horizon." You wonder if Kythie's curse is really enough to prompt all of this... | I am glad things are back to normal in that regard. There was a while where I was concerned that a rift was forming between church and throne." Yeah, she's not the only one happy to see things back to normal around here... ] "So you've never talked with any of them?" "Mmm, I've spoken with Princess Kythie once or twice. She has a passing interest in magic, I understand, and I helped her find a few obscure tomes." Tomes? That's a new one. "Like what?" "Alkezhar's Dreamsong of the Dragon Queens, and Gunvaldsen's The Power of the Mind Unleashed. She's a curious girl; those books are almost never even asked for, and even more rarely released." "Did she say what she wanted from them?" Ynes shakes her head. "Only that she was interested in studying the inner workings of the mind." Interesting. You might ask Kythie about that.
rows.translation: 伊内斯摇了摇头：“我是这座神庙的仆从，根本没有资格与那些贵族子弟或他们的家人交谈。虽然他们偶尔会来拜访我们，但这种拜访绝非自发之举；总会有一位高级神职人员或艾琳娜女士在那里迎接他们，并确保他们的需求得到满足。 [kythie.isCursed |至少，他们曾经是……” “哦？发生了什么变化吗？” “我 shouldn’t gossip（我不应该闲聊的）。” 你探过桌子，说道： [ynes.fucked |轻抚着伊妮斯的脸颊，说道：“来吧，有什么坏处呢？ |你向她露出了最让人安心的微笑：“我不会告诉别人的。 ] 伊内斯叹了口气：“嗯，其实也没人要求我保持沉默，但是……”她稍微压低了声音，“皇宫几乎每天都会派使者去寺庙，而艾琳娜总是把他们打发回去。她一直埋头读书，拒绝任何人打扰，就连王子们也不例外。 许多其他的阿斯特里达也处于同样的困境中，而圣会的成员们也被从国外召回，前来协助他们。这种情况令人担忧。我害怕，一定有什么可怕的事情即将发生。 你不禁会想，难道凯西的诅咒真的足以引发这一切吗？.. | 我很高兴在这方面一切都已经恢复正常了。有那么一段时间，我真的很担心教会与王权之间会出现裂痕。 是啊，这里有很多人都很高兴看到一切恢复了正常…… ] 所以你从来没有和他们中的任何一个人谈过话？” “嗯，我和凯西公主聊过一两次。我知道她对魔法有点兴趣，所以我帮她找了一些不太常见的魔法典籍。” 魔法典籍？这倒是挺新鲜的。“比如哪些典籍？” “阿尔克扎尔的魔法典籍。 龙后们的梦之歌以及冈瓦尔德森的 释放心灵的力量她是个充满好奇心的女孩；那些书籍几乎从来没人会主动要求阅读，更少有人会去借阅它们。” “她有没有说过自己想用那些书籍来研究什么？” 伊内斯摇了摇头：“她只是表示自己对研究人类大脑的运作机制很感兴趣。” 有意思。你可以去问问凯西关于这个话题的意见。

"Alkezhar's Dreamsong of the Dragon Queens, and Gunvaldsen's The Power of the Mind Unleashed. She's a curious girl; those books are almost never even asked for, and even more rarely released." "Did she say what she wanted from them?" Ynes shakes her head. "Only that she was interested in studying the inner workings of the mind." Interesting. You might ask Kythie about that. '

rows.translation: 王室家族

rows.original: [vasko.mdb |"Ah, the lord Magister — well, not lord, I should say. Magister Vasko is as common-born as I, and clearly far more successful." Ynes laughs, resting her chin in her hand. "He's a talented man, truly gifted with sorcery. If he were a bit more sociable, he'd be the darling of the temple, I'm sure." "He does speak highly of you," you say. "I'm sure. He's certainly made no shortage of overtures towards me..." You raise a brow. "Have you ever... "No! No, no. Not like that. It's not what you're thinking: he wants to study my tattoos, the magics writ on my flesh. Though I am sure he'd like to do more than that, given the chance. [ynes.fucked |Don't worry, he's not my type. |Though poor news for him on both fronts, in any case. ]" "Any particular reason why he's been after you?" Ynes just shrugs. "What magician worth his salt wouldn't be, in his shoes? I'm sure a servant of the realm can make use of any sort of magic, even the darkest, for the good of the realm. Still, Lady Eirina has commanded me not to share myself with him, and so I will do as my mistress commands." Now that's strange. "Why would Eirina want to keep the Court Magister from learning your magic?" "She didn't say, and I didn't ask. My mistress's command is good enough for me. He's had to settle for what's in the archive, the same as any other supplicant to the temple." "Hmm, did he ever check out anything particularly unusual?" "From the archive?" Ynes asks, tapping her chin. "It is hard to describe anything I tend to as usual in the first place. Magister Vasko took an intense interest in illusion magic about a year ago — the sort of spellcraft used to create phantasms and manipulate perception. He said he wanted to create a magical body-double of Prince Evander to foil assassins. Frankly, I didn't think Tychris had to worry about such things, but I suppose I wouldn't know..." It didn't that long ago... |"Oh, poor Vasko... I cannot imagine what seized upon him to turn against the realm as he did. I still don't know the full details of his crime — I understand you have much to do with his arrest, though. Well done. [pc.ra astrida |I can see why you were selected for the Order! |You'd make a fine Astrida. ]" "How has he been coping with Dreamlessness?" you ask. "Do you see him often?" Ynes makes a noncommittal noise. "On occasion. We're not all some fraternity here, you know. If anything, I'm sure the church would prefer we all keep our distance from each other. Still, I saw him after the ritual. He was in shambles. I've never seen a grown man sobbing like that before. I... I felt bad for him. But I hope he'll come to learn that this is for the best. That the fetters of Dreamlessness will liberate him from whatever darkness consumed his heart." "What will the temple do with him, do you think?" "We Dreamless serve," she answers evenly. "He had a sharp mind for alchemy, clearly. The Order Astrida has an infinite hunger for potions and decoctions in pursuit of their martial duty. He won't lack for purpose, surely." Surely. |"Ah, yes, I heard about his — her? — transformation. An unfortunately common fate for magicians that specialize in transmutation magic. The field earns its reputation as the most complex and dangerous of all magics for a very good reason, if even a sorcerer of such great ability succumbs to its treachery." Well, if your deception's fooled even the Temple of Nareva's intellectuals, you're doing something right. "How's the city taken the news?" you ask. "The temple's got to have its ear to the ground, right?" Ynes sighs. "In truth, I feel that it's been met with indifference. Some jubilation and humor, even. Vasko was not a popular man; he was elevated to his position against the city's traditions. A commoner having the princep's ear is unheard of, and now things will soon return to the status quo the nobility is more comfortable with. A shame, if you ask me, but I am sure Prince Evander is glad to be rid of the nagging he must get from his barons. Although..." "Go on." "Well, there really was something to be said for having a common man in such a position of power. While I am sure the elite of the city care little for it one way or another, seeing a farmer's son rise to the palace's council inspired many young boys and girls to take more of an interest in their educations; we'd seen a swell of commonborn applying for apprenticeships with the lesser mages of the city, or with the temple's priests. Perhaps I'm imagining things, but it feels like that's already dying off..." [party.has viv |Viv frowns. "That's the real shame of it all." ] ]
rows.translation: [vasko.mdb |啊，那位大法师——嗯，其实也不是那么回事。 领主我得说，瓦斯科大师和我一样出身平凡，但他的成就显然要远大于我。”伊内斯笑着，用手托着下巴，“他是个有才华的人，真的在魔法方面有着与生俱来的天赋。如果他再善于与人交往一些，我相信他会成为神殿里最受宠的人。他确实对你评价很高，”你说。 “我当然相信。他确实对我表现出了很多好意……” 你挑了挑眉毛：“你有没有……” “没有！ 不，不是那样的。其实并不是你想的那样：他只是想研究我的纹身，那些刻在我皮肤上的魔法图案。不过，如果有机会的话，我敢肯定他肯定还想做更多的事情。 [ynes.fucked |别担心，他不是我喜欢的类型。 |无论如何，这两件事对他来说都是坏消息。 ]他之所以一直追着你，有什么特别的理由吗？” 伊内斯只是耸了耸肩：“换作是任何一个称得上是真正魔术师的人，处在他的位置上，不也会这么做吗？我相信，只要是这个王国的仆从，为了王国的利益，就应该能够使用任何形式的魔法，哪怕是最邪恶的那种魔法。不过， 艾琳娜女士命令我不要与他发生关系。那么，我就按照我的女主人的命令去做吧。” 这可真奇怪。“艾琳娜为什么想阻止宫廷法师了解你的魔法呢？” “她没有说明原因，我也没有去问。我的女主人的命令对我来说就已经足够了。 他只能选择档案里现有的东西，和其他向神庙祈求帮助的人一样。” “嗯，他有没有查阅过什么特别不寻常的东西？” “从档案里吗？”伊内斯托着下巴问道。 我倾向于用这样的词语来描述它，但实际上很难找到恰当的表达方式…… 平常的 首先，大约一年前，瓦斯科大师对幻术产生了浓厚的兴趣——那种用于制造幻影、操控人们感知的魔法。他说他想要为埃万德王子制造一个魔法分身，以便用来阻止刺客的袭击。说实话，我原本以为提克里斯不必担心这类事情，但我想，我也不一定了解内情吧…… |哦，可怜的瓦斯科……我实在无法想象是什么驱使他背叛了王国。我至今还不清楚他犯下那些罪行的全部细节——不过，我知道你在他的被捕过程中起到了重要的作用。干得不错。 [pc.ra astrida |我明白为什么你会被选中加入这个团队了！ |你绝对会成为一名出色的阿斯特里达。 ]他是怎么应对‘无梦状态’的？”你问道，“你经常见到他吗？” 伊内斯含糊地回应道：“偶尔会见到。你知道的，我们并不是什么关系亲密的团体。事实上，我觉得教会更希望我们彼此保持距离。 尽管如此，仪式结束后我还是见到了他。他整个人都崩溃了。我从未见过一个成年男人哭得如此伤心。我……为他感到难过。但我希望他最终能明白，这一切都是为了他好。希望“无梦的枷锁”能将他从那些吞噬他心灵的黑暗中解放出来。你觉得这座寺庙会对他做什么呢？” “我们无梦者只是服从命令，”她平静地回答，“显然，他在炼金术方面有着极高的天赋。而阿斯特里达教团为了履行他们的战斗使命，对各种药剂和汤剂有着无尽的需求。 他肯定不会缺乏目标或方向。 |啊，是的，我听说过他的——她的？——转变。对于那些专精于变形魔法的法师来说，这确实是一种常见的命运。变形魔法之所以被称为所有魔法中最复杂、最危险的，是有充分理由的；就连能力如此强大的巫师，也会屈服于它的诡计。 嗯，如果你的欺骗连纳雷瓦神庙的智者们都能上当受骗，那说明你确实做对了一些事情。 “城里的人对这个消息有什么反应？”你问道，“神庙肯定有消息渠道，对吧？英尼斯叹了口气：“事实上，我觉得人们对这件事反应冷淡，甚至还有一些人对此表示欢笑和幽默。瓦斯科并不是一个受欢迎的人；他能够获得这样的地位，其实是违背了这座城市的传统。一个平民能够得到王子的重视，这种事以前从未发生过，而现在，一切很快就会恢复到贵族们更满意的现状。 在我看来，这确实挺遗憾的，但我相信埃万德王子一定会为摆脱那些贵族们不断的烦扰而感到高兴。不过…… 继续说吧。” “嗯，让一个普通人担任这样的权力职位，确实有其积极意义。虽然我敢肯定城里的精英们对此并不在意，但看到一个农民的儿子能够进入宫廷议会，确实激励了许多年轻男女更加重视自己的教育；我们也注意到，越来越多出身平凡的人开始申请成为这座城市里地位较低的法师或寺庙神父的学徒。 也许是我多虑了，但感觉这种现象已经在逐渐消失了…… [party.has viv |薇芙皱起了眉头：“这才是这一切真正可悲的地方。 ] ]
rows.raw: ' [vasko.mdb |"Ah, the lord Magister — well, not lord, I should say. Magister Vasko is as common-born as I, and clearly far more successful." Ynes laughs, resting her chin in her hand. "He's a talented man, truly gifted with sorcery. If he were a bit more sociable, he'd be the darling of the temple, I'm sure." "He does speak highly of you," you say. "I'm sure. He's certainly made no shortage of overtures towards me..." You raise a brow. "Have you ever... "No! No, no. Not like that. It's not what you're thinking: he wants to study my tattoos, the magics writ on my flesh. Though I am sure he'd like to do more than that, given the chance. [ynes.fucked |Don't worry, he's not my type. |Though poor news for him on both fronts, in any case. ]" "Any particular reason why he's been after you?" Ynes just shrugs. "What magician worth his salt wouldn't be, in his shoes? I'm sure a servant of the realm can make use of any sort of magic, even the darkest, for the good of the realm. Still, Lady Eirina has commanded me not to share myself with him, and so I will do as my mistress commands." Now that's strange. "Why would Eirina want to keep the Court Magister from learning your magic?" "She didn't say, and I didn't ask. My mistress's command is good enough for me. He's had to settle for what's in the archive, the same as any other supplicant to the temple." "Hmm, did he ever check out anything particularly unusual?" "From the archive?" Ynes asks, tapping her chin. "It is hard to describe anything I tend to as usual in the first place. Magister Vasko took an intense interest in illusion magic about a year ago — the sort of spellcraft used to create phantasms and manipulate perception. He said he wanted to create a magical body-double of Prince Evander to foil assassins. Frankly, I didn't think Tychris had to worry about such things, but I suppose I wouldn't know..." It didn't that long ago... |"Oh, poor Vasko... I cannot imagine what seized upon him to turn against the realm as he did. I still don't know the full details of his crime — I understand you have much to do with his arrest, though. Well done. [pc.ra astrida |I can see why you were selected for the Order! |You'd make a fine Astrida. ]" "How has he been coping with Dreamlessness?" you ask. "Do you see him often?" Ynes makes a noncommittal noise. "On occasion. We're not all some fraternity here, you know. If anything, I'm sure the church would prefer we all keep our distance from each other. Still, I saw him after the ritual. He was in shambles. I've never seen a grown man sobbing like that before. I... I felt bad for him. But I hope he'll come to learn that this is for the best. That the fetters of Dreamlessness will liberate him from whatever darkness consumed his heart." "What will the temple do with him, do you think?" "We Dreamless serve," she answers evenly. "He had a sharp mind for alchemy, clearly. The Order Astrida has an infinite hunger for potions and decoctions in pursuit of their martial duty. He won't lack for purpose, surely." Surely. |"Ah, yes, I heard about his — her? — transformation. An unfortunately common fate for magicians that specialize in transmutation magic. The field earns its reputation as the most complex and dangerous of all magics for a very good reason, if even a sorcerer of such great ability succumbs to its treachery." Well, if your deception's fooled even the Temple of Nareva's intellectuals, you're doing something right. "How's the city taken the news?" you ask. "The temple's got to have its ear to the ground, right?" Ynes sighs. "In truth, I feel that it's been met with indifference. Some jubilation and humor, even. Vasko was not a popular man; he was elevated to his position against the city's traditions. A commoner having the princep's ear is unheard of, and now things will soon return to the status quo the nobility is more comfortable with. A shame, if you ask me, but I am sure Prince Evander is glad to be rid of the nagging he must get from his barons. Although..." "Go on." "Well, there really was something to be said for having a common man in such a position of power. While I am sure the elite of the city care little for it one way or another, seeing a farmer's son rise to the palace's council inspired many young boys and girls to take more of an interest in their educations; we'd seen a swell of commonborn applying for apprenticeships with the lesser mages of the city, or with the temple's priests. Perhaps I'm imagining things, but it feels like that's already dying off..." [party.has viv |Viv frowns. "That's the real shame of it all." ] ] '

rows.original:
"What do you have in the restricted archives?"
    
    "All manner of tomes, from treatises of war to unique spellbooks, penned by hundreds of different devotees of the goddess. And some that were... recovered... from those who drew the ire of her blessed Astrida. What we have here is dangerous, but not forbidden; it is my job to watch over the material and decide who can or cannot examine each tome. You, [ynes.fucked
        |my dear 
    ]Champion, have been given leave to peruse the whole of my collection.
rows.translation:
那些被封存的档案里有什么？”

“里面有各种各样的书籍，从战争论著到独一无二的法术书，这些都是由数百位这位女神的虔诚信徒撰写的。还有一些书籍……是从那些惹恼了她神圣的阿斯特里达的人手中夺回来的。我们这里收藏的这些资料确实很危险，但并不属于被禁止的内容；我的职责就是负责看管这些资料，并决定谁可以阅读哪些书籍。你…… [ynes.fucked
        |亲爱的 
    ]冠军，你已经被允许仔细查阅我的全部收藏品了。

rows.raw:
"
    You pull one of the myriad tomes off the temple's sprawling shelves and hold it up to Ynes. She gives you a nod, and then gently nudges the donation box on her desk. You get the hint and drop a handful of electrum hawks into it on your way past her[ynes.fucked
        | — and you donate a kiss on her supple lips while you're at it
    ]. You can't exactly leave the temple with such a rare artifact, but lucky for you there's no shortage of reading space in here. You find an empty chair and dig into the tome you've chosen.
    
    [party.has cait
        |Cait gives you a hug from behind. [cait.isNarevan
            |"I've got a few books I've been looking at too. Mind if I join you?"
            
            You smile and scoot over, making room for your kitty-companion to spread her own work out next to you. Her tail coils affectionately around your waist, and you find yourself thankful for her warmth against the pervasive chill of the stone temple.
            |"You have fun. I'm gonna go make some hapless nerd's dreams come true." She [cait.isLover
                |gives you a kiss on the cheek
                |gives you a wink
            ] and prowls off towards the loneliest, saddest looking salamander in the temple. 
        ]
    ]
    
    [party.has arona
        |Arona groans. "This place is a drag. I'm gonna go so see if there's a tavern in the town. [arona.isPregnant
            |Not gonna drink or nothing, but I can probably find my way into a fistfight.
            |Later, nerd.
        ]"
    ]
    
    [party.has viv
        |[viv.isDemon
            |
            |Viviane drops down into a chair across from you with a collection of tomes of her own, clearly happy to have the opportunity to get down and dirty with some ancient texts.
        ]
    ]
    
    It's always quiet in the temple, with only the hushed whispers of acolytes and educators piercing the studious silence the clergy demands between services. The loudest sound is the steady hum of a choir vocalizing softly somewhere out of sight, and the occasional ring of ritual gongs. It's easy to tune out the rest of the world and lose yourself in your studies... 
    "

rows.translation: 下一个

rows.original:
... And then the next thing you know, a rooster is crowing somewhere outside.
        
        Several empty cups and a half-eaten bowl of stew surround you, [party.has cait
            |[cait.isNarevan
                |as does a very sleepy Cait, face-down in a book.
                |and Cait has come back from her escapades and sprawled out next to you.
            ]
            | and there's a couple of younger acolytes at nearby tables who're in no better shape.
        ] This is hardly unusual, here, you realize: there are senior priests moving amongst your fellow wick-burners, bringing plates of bread and cheese and cups of tea. 
        
        You take one such cup gratefully, rubbing your burning eyes. Gods, where did the time go...
        
        Yawning, you close the tome you'd been studying. It's hard to believe, but you devoured it from cover to cover overnight... and you're pretty sure you were getting near the end of a second readthrough when the academic haze wore off. You push yourself up and take the book back to Ynes's desk. 
        
        "Good morning, [pc.name]," she says, still rubbing the sleep from her eyes. "Learn much?"
        
        You can't help but nod. You've definitely crammed your brain, alright.
rows.translation:
然后，不知不觉间，外面有公鸡开始打鸣了。

周围摆着几个空杯子，还有半碗吃剩的炖菜。 [party.has cait
            |[cait.isNarevan
                |同样地，那个困倦至极的凯特也趴在书上睡着了。
                |而凯特也结束了她的冒险之旅，现在正躺在你身边。
            ]
            | 在附近的几张桌子上，也有一些年纪较小的学徒，他们的状况也好不到哪里去。
        ] 这种情况在这里其实并不罕见：你会看到一些资深祭司在你们这些使用灯芯燃烧燃料的人中间走动，他们端着装满面包和奶酪的盘子，以及茶杯。你感激地接过其中一杯茶，同时揉了揉自己发红的眼睛。 天啊，时间都去哪儿了……

你打着哈欠，合上了那本你一直在研究的书。真难以相信，你竟然一整晚都在读它……而且你很确定，在那种学术研究的专注状态消退之后，你差不多已经读到第二遍的结尾了。 你用力站起来，把书放回伊内斯的桌子上。

“早上好， [pc.name]嗯，”她边说边揉着惺忪的双眼，“学到了很多吗？”

你不禁点了点头。确实，你把很多知识都记在了脑子里，没错。

rows.original: "Oh, my, desperate for my touch again, are you?" Ynes teases with a click of her tongue. Her hazel eyes narrow with a predatory glint. "Good. I've an itch that needs scratched as well. Follow me." She stands, [ynes.pregStage 0 2 3 |taking your hand and leading you towards the rectory door |one hand on her growing belly and the other on your waist, leading you towards the rectory door |cradling her enormous belly. With a nod, she guides you towards the rectory door ]. You follow along, paying more attention to the sensual sway of Ynes's hips beneath her dark robes than to your surroundings. All you really perceive is slate-gray stone passages that eventually give way to wood paneling and carpets once you pass through an erstwhile locked door. [flags.YNES_FLIRT |The moment the door's locked back up behind you, you're watching Ynes shrugging her holy robes off, giving you an eyeful of lilac tattoos whirling around pale, freckled flesh. "That's better," she sighs, adjusting her long, silk gloves. "Ah. Admiring already, are you?" "How can I not?" you say, [ynes.pregStage 2 |closing the distance and putting your hands on her [ynes.belly]. "You're gorgeous." She rolls her eyes, but rests her hands over yours, guiding them around her baby bump. Slowly, though, she draws you upwards, from belly to breast. Your fingers sink into the softness of her, disappearing into tattooed titty. "Mmm, there we are. I know what kind of [pc.mf |man |girl ] you are." She smirks, pushing her chest into your hands. "No need to be around the bush with me. Tell me what you desire, my Champion." |drinking in the sight of her. Ynes laughs indulgently and closes the distance, clasping your hands and guiding them to her breasts where they belong. You instinctively squeeze down, sinking your fingers into her. Gods, she's so soft. Your fingers practically disappear into her chest. "You don't need to hold back around me, my Champion. Let's indulge those desires of yours, hm?" ] |"Woah," you say, finally prying your eyes off Ynes's backside to look around. This room is luxurious, dominated by a four-posted and canopied bed, surrounded by thick Jassiran carpets and masterfully carved oak furniture. Desk, wardrobe, two nightstands with glass lanterns flickering with arcane lights... there's even a standing mirror, through which you see Ynes shrug off her vestment robes, letting you get an eyeful of the swirling lilac tattoos scrawled across every inch of bare flesh below her collar. [ynes.pregStage 3 |It's impossible to draw your gaze off where they're stretching around her gravid middle, wrapping your child in matrices of arcane runes. ] "Like the view?" Ynes says with a smirk, adjusting the long lace gloves stretching up her pale arms. The act serves to press her shoulders together, doing absolutely jaw-dropping things to her already alluring bosom. You cough, adjusting your own clothing. "You've got a nice place here." "For a slave, you mean? Hah. It's Eirina's. But she barely uses it. She certainly won't mind if we do. Now..." rows.original: You've barely set foot in the cold, quiet sanctum of the temple-library before a pair of hands are grabbing yours, and you're being dragged from the doors to a coat closet. There's barely enough room for two bodies in there, particularly when [pc.cupRange G |you're both this absurdly curvy! "Ynes?" |one of them is as absurdly curvy as Ynes. ] "We need to talk, pet." [ynes.pregStage 2 |In such close confines, you can't avoid seeing, and feeling, exactly what she's talking about — her stomach's pushing up against the front of her robes, almost matching the enormous swell of her breasts. "Y-you're pregnant!" "Yes, very observant. And you're very, very late to the announcement. Tsk." |"What's going on?" you ask, putting your hands on Ynes's waist. "Everything alright?" "No. Well, that's not... no. I'm not sure I am alright. I'm..." Ynes takes a deep breath, closing her eyes. "[pc.name], I'm pregnant." ] Ynes guides your hands to her [ynes.belly]. "It's yours. There's no doubt about that. I suppose I never really believed our actions would actually have such... inescapable consequences. But here we are. And now, I need your support." You're still in the shock of the moment — if you didn't have a wall for support you're not sure you'd still be standing. "Wha-what can I do?" "I..." Ynes shrugs. "I don't know. This is all new to me. At my age, I- I'd accepted motherhood was a dream long behind me. This is a surprise, to be sure. But not an entirely unwelcome one. So long as you intend to remain a part of this child's life. A Dreamless thrall wouldn't be my first choice for a mother, and a spinster one even moreso." You take a breath yourself. "What does your being a thrall mean for our baby?" "That is up to Eirina. She is my mistress, and what becomes of my — our — child is up to her. It has been customary for more than a century for a slave's master to free her children upon birth, but de jure she could-" "She wouldn't," you assure her, before she can fall into the grip of self-doubt. "Eirina cares about you. That's obvious." "Yes, yes. I... yes, of course. You're right. But it is still my prerogative to be concerned. I have to be, for this child. Our child. I... this is very new to me. All of this." You smile and squeeze her hands. "We'll make it work." "Yes, we will." Ynes forces a smile, takes a step closer, and gives you a tentative kiss. "I've never been one for romance, [pc.name]. I don't think I've ever had a stable relationship like you- like most people have. But I will make an effort, if you will." You squeeze the witch, pulling her close and caressing her belly. You can certainly start now, with a much more intense kiss. Ynes squirms, sighs, and finally reciprocates. "Enough, enough. Don't get too ahead of yourself, pet. I said I am still unused to this! And I must get back to work before I am missed. We will talk more, yes?" You nod and open the door for her. "Soon."

rows.original: You've barely gotten past the library door before you see Ynes marching towards you, frowning so deep that if looks could kill, you'd be stone cold on the floor already. "Ynes?" "You jackass," she hisses, loud as she can without breaking the quiet of the library. "It's been months!" You put your hands up. "I've-" She grabs you by the [pc.armor]. "What could possibly have taken so long that you missed my entire pregnancy!? Real love-them-and-leave-them, aren't you?" Your face pales. "You... what... again!?" "Yes, again!" she seethes. "And you cared not a whit! Not one word from you for months!" [pc.isDK |"Get over it," you groan. "I'm here now, aren't I?" |"I'm sorry! I had no idea!" ] Ynes glowers, finally sighs, and puts her hands on her hips. "Well, you are here now, and that is what matters. Give me a minute to calm down, and we can start again. You should come and see your children. They've missed you." "I'll do that..."

One of the Astrida guards catches your shoulder as you head through the door, pulling you aside. "Ynes asked me to keep an eye out for you, [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |Brother
            |Sister
        ]
        |Champion
    ]. I understand congratulations are in order."
    
    "Congratulations!" the other guard laughs, slapping your back. "Way to go, [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |Brother
            |Sister
        ]
        |Champion
    ]!"
    
    You know what that means! Hurrying downstairs, you wind your way through the servants' quarters, collecting more congratulations from the other Dreamless in the temple's service on the way, until you arrive at Ynes's door. You give it a knock and step inside, one of your rare glimpses at the witch's cluttered private chambers. [ynes.kidName] is in his cradle, and Ynes herself is in her bed, holding another bundle of joy to her breast. 
    
    "Well, well, look who it is. Your [pc.mf
        |daddy
        |mommy
    ]!" she says with an uncharacteristically warm smile, rocking the newborn back and forth. "Missing the festivities as usual. [pc.HeShe]'s lucky that I'm getting used to this." 
    
    You take a seat on the edge of the bed, wrapping an arm around the witch's slender shoulders — just about the only slender thing about her, especially after repeated pregnancy. Now, with a baby on her breast, she's a positive idol of fertility wrapped in alluring lilac. Ynes gives you a pretty smile, nuzzling up against you. "It's a [rand
        |boy
        |girl
    ]," she says, stroking fuzzy red hair. 
    
    "Another little Ynes running around. You both look good."
    
    "An easy labor, Nareva be praised. Even still, I'm sure she could hear me howling from her lair!" She looks proud of herself all the same. "I'll be back on my feet soon, don't you worry. And I already talked with Eirina; she's a good friend as always. Sent her intent to free our child to the Princeps months ago."
    
    "That's good of her," you say. "Maybe someday she'll-"
    
    Ynes stops you with a finger on your lips. "Tsk. No. Don't. I'm happy where I am. With our little family. I wonder if you're done growing it, hm?"
    
    You slip your hand a little downwards from her shoulder. "You just get your rest for now."
    
    "Oh, I will. And when I'm done, I'll be sure to repay the pains of labor in kind. But for now... I am content, [pc.name]. And I am glad you're here."
rows.translation:
当你穿过大门时，一名阿斯特里达的守卫抓住你的肩膀，将你拉到一边。“伊内斯让我照看一下你。 [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |兄弟
            |姐姐
        ]
        |冠军
    ]我明白现在应该表示祝贺了。”

“恭喜你！”另一名守卫笑着拍了拍你的背，“干得真棒！ [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |兄弟
            |姐姐
        ]
        |冠军
    ]你明白这意味着什么吧！”你急忙下楼，穿过仆人们的住处，一路上还收到了寺庙里其他从事服务工作的人们的祝贺，直到你来到了伊妮丝的门前。你敲了敲门，走了进去——这是你难得有机会看到这位女巫杂乱无章的私人房间。 [ynes.kidName] 婴儿还躺在摇篮里，而伊内斯自己则躺在床上，怀中抱着另一个刚刚出生的小生命。

“哇，看看是谁来了。是你的…… [pc.mf
        |爸爸
        |妈妈
    ]真遗憾，”她带着一种不同寻常的温暖笑容说道，一边轻轻摇晃着新生儿，“又像往常一样错过了这些庆祝活动。 [pc.HeShe]幸好我正在慢慢适应这一切。”

你坐在床边，将手臂环绕在那位女巫纤细的肩上——这几乎是她身上为数不多的纤细部位了，尤其是在经历了多次怀孕之后。如今，她怀抱着婴儿，仿佛成了象征生育力的象征，浑身散发着迷人的淡紫色光芒。伊内斯向你露出了甜美的微笑，然后紧紧依偎在你身边：“这真是…… [rand
        |男孩
        |女孩
    ]真是又一个小伊内斯诞生了，”她一边说，一边轻抚着自己那头柔软的红发。

“你们两个看起来都很好。”

“分娩过程很顺利，娜雷瓦真是值得称赞。不过，我敢肯定，她肯定能听到我在洞穴里发出的叫声！”她依然为自己感到骄傲。 我很快就会重新站起来的，不用担心。而且我已经和艾琳娜谈过了；她一如既往地是个好朋友。几个月前，她就向王子表达了想要救出我们孩子的意愿。”

“她真是太好了，”你说道，“也许有一天她……
    
    伊内斯用手指轻按在你的嘴唇上，制止了你。“嘘，不要这样。我现在很开心，和我们的小家庭在一起。我在想，你是不是已经不再想要扩大这个家庭了，嗯？”

你轻轻地将手从她的肩膀上往下移了移。“你现在只需要好好休息一下就好。哦，我会的。等我完成后，一定会用实际行动来回报所有的辛勤付出。不过现在……我已经很满足了。 [pc.name]我很高兴你在这里。
rows.raw:
'
    One of the Astrida guards catches your shoulder as you head through the door, pulling you aside. "Ynes asked me to keep an eye out for you, [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |Brother
            |Sister
        ]
        |Champion
    ]. I understand congratulations are in order."
    
    "Congratulations!" the other guard laughs, slapping your back. "Way to go, [pc.ra astrida
        |[pc.mf
            |Brother
            |Sister
        ]
        |Champion
    ]!"
    
    You know what that means! Hurrying downstairs, you wind your way through the servants' quarters, collecting more congratulations from the other Dreamless in the temple's service on the way, until you arrive at Ynes's door. You give it a knock and step inside, one of your rare glimpses at the witch's cluttered private chambers. [ynes.kidName] is in his cradle, and Ynes herself is in her bed, holding another bundle of joy to her breast. 
    
    "Well, well, look who it is. Your [pc.mf
        |daddy
        |mommy
    ]!" she says with an uncharacteristically warm smile, rocking the newborn back and forth. "Missing the festivities as usual. [pc.HeShe]'s lucky that I'm getting used to this."
